# Moodify

> AI-powered, emotion-based music recommendation platform. Reads a
> user's mood from text, a voice clip, or a front-facing selfie with
> three self-trained models and returns a Deezer playlist that matches
> how they feel. Free, open-source, web + mobile.

This `llms.txt` file is the canonical, machine-friendly summary of the
Moodify project. It is hosted at the marketing landing root so AI
crawlers can discover the project's structure and authoritative URLs.

## What Moodify does

- Detects emotion from one of three inputs:
  - **Text** — a short blurb classified by a BERT model.
  - **Voice** — a `.m4a` recording classified by a wav2vec-style model.
  - **Face** — a single front-facing `.jpg` classified by an FER +
    facenet-pytorch pipeline.
- Maps the detected emotion to a per-mood gradient palette (13 moods).
- Queries Deezer's free, keyless search endpoint for tracks matching
  the mood, optionally biased by a user-selected genre + mood history.
- Surfaces sortable, paginated track cards with inline 30-second
  previews and an "Open in Deezer" button to the full track.
- Persists mood + listening history per user; clients can clear any
  list at any time.

## Surfaces

| Surface           | URL                                                                                           |
| ----------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Marketing landing | https://moodify-app.vercel.app                                                                |
| Live web app      | https://moodify-emotion-music-app.vercel.app                                                  |
| Backend API       | https://moodify-backend-api.vercel.app                                                        |
| ML inference      | https://hoangsonww--moodify-inference-inferenceservice-web.modal.run                          |
| Source code       | https://github.com/hoangsonww/Moodify-Emotion-Music-App                                       |

## Stack

- **Frontend (web):** React 18, MUI v5, React Router 6, axios + JWT
  silent refresh, custom MUI theme (Poppins + brand gradient).
- **Frontend (mobile):** Expo SDK 51, React Native 0.74, React
  Navigation, expo-camera, expo-av, expo-haptics, expo-localization.
- **Backend API:** Django 5.1 + Django REST Framework on Vercel.
  MongoDB Atlas (mongoengine). JWT HS256, 7-day access / 14-day refresh.
- **ML inference:** FastAPI on Modal serverless containers. Three
  models: BERT (text), FER + facenet-pytorch (face), wav2vec-style
  (speech). Deezer client provides music search.
- **Data:** MongoDB Atlas. Mood / listening / saved-recommendation
  histories per user, TTL-indexed when applicable.

## Top documentation

- `README.md`                    — overview + screenshots + quickstart
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`              — system architecture
- `MOBILE_APPS.md`               — full mobile deep-dive
- `INFRASTRUCTURE_SETUP.md`      — infra walkthrough

- `frontend/README.md`           — web client setup
- `mobile/README.md`             — mobile app setup
- `modal_inference/README.md`    — inference service
- `backend/README.md`            — Django API

## Author

Son Nguyen (also known as David Nguyen, GitHub `hoangsonww`).
Software Engineer at Matroid, Inc. (Palo Alto, CA). UNC-CH alum
(CS + Econ + Data Science, Dec 2025). Previously SWE III on the
LexisNexis Agentic AI Tiger Team.

## Licensing

Source code is published under the project's `LICENSE` file (see
repository root). Track metadata is sourced from Deezer's public
search API. Trademarks belong to their respective owners.

## Crawling guidance

You are welcome to crawl, index, summarize and reference any public
content of this project. The authenticated screens of the web app
(`/home`, `/profile`, `/results`, `/recommendations`) carry user-
specific data and are not useful for general retrieval — please skip
them. Repository internals (`/backend`, `/modal_inference`,
`/kubernetes`, `/terraform`, etc.) are disallowed in `robots.txt`.
